title: Pass 5.D.2: ferrotorch (umbrella) pythia_real missing #[ignore]; validate_vs_pytorch zero assertions
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch (umbrella) audit, TEST_INTEGRITY.

- tests/pythia_real.rs:184 — #[test] fn test_pythia_70m_training_benchmark not #[ignore]-gated despite header line 8 saying users should opt in with --ignored. Default cargo test triggers ~26M-param Pythia-70M autograd at every step → CI OOM/timeout risk.
- tests/pythia_real.rs:1-5 docstring is fully aspirational: claims to load HF safetensors and compare against PyTorch reference; line 196 actually builds PythiaModel::new with random init.
- tests/validate_vs_pytorch.rs:446-542 — test_speed_comparison has zero assertions; prints ratio table to stderr and exits success regardless of measured perf. NOT registered in Cargo.toml as [[test]] but still discovered by Cargo's filename-based discovery.

Acceptance:
- pythia_real: add #[ignore = "..."] OR rename file/function to require --ignored; align doc with what the test actually does (random-init or real-load — pick one)
- validate_vs_pytorch: replace prints with hard assertions on perf-ratio thresholds; OR move to benches/ if perf-only.
- Register or remove validate_vs_pytorch from Cargo.toml [[test]] block.
